steps.title: Use the mixing mechanism
steps.content: The research team's conclusion depends on a transition at 3.2 billion years ago. Before that point, the stagnant lid regime kept the lithosphere and mantle from interacting. After the transition to plate tectonics, lithospheric and mantle material could mix.

steps.title: Look for dated mixing
steps.content: The strongest support would show material moving from the lithosphere into mantle-derived rocks after the proposed transition date. That would connect the chemical evidence directly to the mechanism described in the passage instead of merely comparing rock quantities or diversity.

steps.title: Eliminate weaker findings
steps.content: Choice A compares how many rocks of each type exist, not whether their materials mixed. Choice B compares compositional diversity before the date. Choice C says older lithospheric rocks are more similar to mantle-derived rocks, which points the wrong way. Lithospheric material appearing in younger mantle-derived rocks directly supports post-transition mixing. The answer is D.
